title: astragalus muelleri subsp. etruscus
intro: Astragalus muelleri subsp. etruscus, also known as Etruscan milkvetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family. It is a perennial herb with a long history of medicinal use, prized for its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's medicinal properties have been traditionally used to support immune function, reduce stress and fatigue, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions. Astragalus muelleri subsp. etruscus can be found in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits, including improved overall well-being and enhanced resistance to illness.
